assertions.title: Settings page loads system configuration

steps.expected: Settings page loads with GET /api/v1/admin/settings returning 200
steps.observed: Page loaded successfully. GET /api/v1/admin/settings returned 200 with valid JSON data. Four XHR requests to /api/v1/admin/settings were observed in network log.

steps.action: Verify sections display: General, Approvals, Email, Advanced
steps.expected: All four sections visible with populated values
steps.observed: All four sections (General, Approvals, Email, Advanced) are displayed with correct section headers and descriptions.

steps.expected: Fields show values from API response (company name, timezone, currency, SMTP settings, etc.)
steps.observed: BUG: Most field values display '—' (dash fallback) instead of actual API data. The API returns data with different field names than the frontend expects. API fields: autoApprovalEnabled, periodLockEnforced, exportApprovalRequired, mailEnabled, mailFromAddress, allowedOrigins. Frontend expects: autoApproveThreshold, periodLockEnabled, emailNotifications, smtpFromEmail, corsAllowedOrigins, companyName, currency, timezone, dateFormat. Only the boolean toggles for 'Export Approval Required: Disabled' and 'Period Lock: Disabled' render (though Period Lock should show 'Enabled' since API returns periodLockEnforced=true).

evidence.network: GET /api/v1/admin/settings → 200 (verified via curl). API response: { success: true, data: { allowedOrigins: ['http://localhost:3002'], autoApprovalEnabled: true, periodLockEnforced: true, exportApprovalRequired: false, mailEnabled: true, mailFromAddress: 'noreply@bigbrightpaints.com', ... } }

assertions.issues: Field name mismatch between backend API response and frontend types. Backend returns camelCase fields (periodLockEnforced, autoApprovalEnabled, mailEnabled, mailFromAddress, allowedOrigins) but frontend expects different names (periodLockEnabled, autoApproveThreshold, emailNotifications, smtpFromEmail, corsAllowedOrigins). Additionally, the API does not return companyName, currency, timezone, or dateFormat fields, so General section fields all show '—'.

steps.action: Check for edit buttons, input fields, or save buttons
steps.expected: No edit buttons, input fields, or save buttons visible on the page
steps.observed: No edit buttons, no input fields (0 found), no save/submit/update/edit/modify buttons found. Only navigation buttons (sidebar, profile menu, theme toggle) present.

steps.action: Verify read-only notice with Lock icon is displayed
steps.expected: Read-only notice with Lock icon visible
steps.observed: Read-only notice displayed: 'Global settings are managed by platform administrators. Contact your platform team to change these values.' with Lock icon (svg.lucide-lock) present.

steps.expected: No PUT or PATCH network requests made
steps.observed: No PUT or PATCH requests found in network log. Only GET requests observed.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-SET-002: read-only mode works correctly), 1 failed (VAL-SET-001: settings page loads but most field values show '—' due to field name mismatch between backend API response and frontend type mapping). The API returns 200 with valid data but the frontend expects different field names.
